content.problemStatement:
Chef likes problems related to numbers a lot. He is generally quite good at solving these kinds of problems, but today he got stuck at one number theory problem, and so he asks your help to solve it.


Given a positive integer **N**, find out how many positive integers **X** satisfy the equation **X** + S(**X**) + S(S(**X**)) = **N**, where S(**X**) denoting sum of digits of **X** in decimal (base 10) representation.
content.inputFormat: The only line of the input contains one integer number - N.
content.outputFormat: Output single integer in a line denoting the count of number of positive integers X satisfying the above given equation.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Only one positive integer X = 2 satisfies the equation X + S(X) + S(S(X)) = 6, as X + S(X) + S(S(X)) = 2 + 2 + 2 = 6.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can iterate over all possible values of X and check if the equation X + S(X) + S(S(X)) = N is satisfied. We can calculate S(X) by converting X to a string and summing up the digits. We can calculate S(S(X)) by converting S(X) to a string and summing up the digits.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) - We are iterating over all possible values of X.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) - We are using a constant amount of space to store the variables.
